desc:
This is where the main channel of Greyflood flows over some rocky outcroppings
in fierce and dangerous rapids. A thick mist limits visibility, and you can
barely outline the silhouette of a cliff far south, and the shores on the
northern and southeastern sides. Everywhere whirlpools and undertows lurk
between the rocks, ready to drag you down into the dark water.
name: Turbulent Rapids of the Greyflood

desc:
The water flows quickly here past a brushy riverbank to the south. East of here
the bank is flat and muddy. To the west the surface of the river looks strange
and uneven; a rushing sound comes from that way, and a mist obscures your view.
name: The Greyflood

desc:
The Greyflood makes a sharp turn west here and disappears below a rocky ledge.
The water is foamy and wild, creating a haze in the air. To the north the water
is even more upset, passing some heavy rapids. East of here is some marshy,
brush-covered ground, and to the south a bluff rises from the riverside.
name: Rapids of the Greyflood

desc:
This outcropping is covered in brush and high grass. North and below the river
makes its way southwest through harsh rapids. Looking east, you see the flat
plains of Dunland stretching out beyond some more brushy land. South, the lands
of Enedwaith lie before your eyes. The brush grows thicker though, eventually
prohibiting travel in that direction.
name: Brushy Bluffs

desc:
This flat, muddy land is watered by the Greyflood a few miles north. Brush and
thick bushes grow here in the rich, black soil. A steep bank, seeming to have
been cut by water, rises up to the south. To the west is dryer, higher ground;
to the east, the vegetation thins out and the plains of Enedwaith begin.
name: Brushy Grassland

desc:
This low riverbank is entangled in thick brush. The ground is muddy, as water
escapes the fast flowing river to the north and east, forming small pools. Some
higher ground is to the south and east, where the plains begin. You hear the
river pass some heavy rapids to the northwest, but all you can see is a thick
haze covering the surface.
name: Brushy Riverbank

desc:
A low swale of grass and reeds extends around you. The ground here is sandy, and
heaps or bars of round, water-worn pebbles can be seen everywhere. To the south,
the ground rises abruptly, and water-worn bluffs can be seen. Here and there is
an ancient trunk, its bark stripped clean, half-buried in the mud and sand.
name: Grassy Floodplain

desc:
The shore here is flat, muddy, and full of bushes and vines. North and west the
Greyflood flows slowly past, while fields and flatlands await to the east and
south. The bank is covered with reeds and cattails, giving home to several
species of insects and fish. The foundation stones of a house and some
outbuildings, long abandoned, and what seems to be the capstone of a well, lie
here, sinking year by year deeper into the mud.
name: Muddy Shoreline

desc:
This is rolling grassland. To the west it drops off rather sharply, and the
ground there is low, damp, and overgrown. The flat grassland continues to the
north and east, rolling towards Eregion.
name: Grassy Flats

desc:
The grass grows thick and dense in these fields, hiding a trail which turns into
a small road as it approaches a little cottage to the west. If you follow it
east you will enter some other grassy fields.
name: A Trail in the Plains

desc:
The grass here is thick and dense. You hear the sound of small insects hiding in
the grass. There is a high fence to the southwest enclosing some kind of lawn.
North, east and south you see more flat land, while the Greyflood can be seen
flowing southward beyond some muddy ground west of here.
name: Thick Grassy Field

desc:
You stand in a small quiet tunnel. In the distance you can hear running water.
Your light throws ugly shadows all over the walls behind you. Looking at the
walls you can see that they have been carved out of the rock and there is water
trickling down the tunnel sides. The tunnel runs east from here.
name: Narrow Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel here is only maybe three feet across at the most. The tunnel being
this narrow forces you to almost walk sideways like a crab as you sidle your way
through the gloom. Off in the distance voices can be heard chattering away, but
you are too far to make out what they are saying. From this point the tunnel
continues east and west.
name: Narrow Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel bends south and west here. Far in the distance you can hear the sound
of running water and some faint voices chattering away. The water is running
down the sides of the tunnel with more vigour than before and it is quite damp
underfoot.
name: Bend in the Tunnel

desc:
There is a slight movement in the air here suggesting that you may be heading
out. From here you can see that the tunnel continues north and south, off into
the darkness. The sound of running water is getting steadily louder as you
continue your journey.
name: A Narrow Tunnel

desc:
You arrive a T-junction in the tunnel. To the east a side tunnel disappears off
into the darkness. As you peer in that direction you realise that you can only
see a few feet in front of your nose. The main tunnel continues north and south.
To the south you can see a small landing lit by a few torches.
name: A Narrow Tunnel

desc:
This tunnel is very narrow and very quiet. Looking at the dust and dirt on the
ground you can only see a few sets of footprints giving the impression that this
tunnel is not used a lot. Looking to the east you can see the tunnel
disappearing off into the gloom.
name: A Deserted Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel walls seem to close in around you here. Further to the east the
ceiling arches down to meet the wall, and a small round opening is set into the
very centre. Closer inspection suggests that the only way that hole leads is
down. The footprints in the dust stop leading east, and turn to the south here.
name: A Deserted Tunnel

desc:
You enter a small room with the main feature being a set of stairs spiralling up
in the centre. Looking at these stairs you can see that they have been worn
smooth by years of feet climbing up and down them. The only exit is back to
north.
name: Base of some Stairs

desc:
A cramped and roughly formed chamber, this room has apparently not been made
with any long-term use in mind. Its floor, walls and ceiling are uneven, and in
places the marks of whatever tool was used to carve this niche still scar the
cold stone. Only around the door is there any sign of precision, and the small
round slab fits perfectly into the hole it has been created to fill. It would
seem that secrecy is the main aim of this place.
name: In a Rough-Hewn Hole

desc:
The tunnel is cramped, with its rough-hewn walls making close approach to one
another in many places. The floor is uneven, with sharp rocks sticking up here
and there, and despite a number of twisted timbers, the roof above you bulges in
several spots, as if on the verge of falling in. A foul, putrescent stench wafts
in from the east, reminiscent of rotting flesh and human waste.
name: A Turning Tunnel

desc:
A foul smell permeates the entire pit, and the floor underfoot is covered in
some sort of thick, soft, moist, rotting matter. Far above your head a rough
opening can be seen, and it would seem that perhaps this hole was made with a
marked lack of care and skill, for occasionally a small stone or a flurry of
dust will tumble down. However, as crude as it may have been, the effort was
sufficient to create a functional refuse pit.
name: Bottom of a Filthy Pit

desc:
This long tunnel has curved walls and such a low ceiling that it almost looks
like a hobbit hole in its shape. But no halfling would live in such a dank and
gloomy location. Water drips down the walls to form small, murky puddles on the
floor, and slimy mould grows everywhere. A foul stench pervades the entire area,
and the faint whisper of fresh air, which emanates from a shaft above your head,
does little to freshen the atmosphere.
name: A Long, Low-Roofed Tunnel

desc:
Here the tunnel turns from west to north. Its smooth, curved walls are almost
covered by a thick coating of green-grey mould, made slick by the chilly water,
which seeps in through hairline cracks near the roof. A slightly rotten odour
emanates from the slime, filling the air around you, but a faint, cool breeze
drifting down from above offers a little relief.
name: A Long, Low-Roofed Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel stretches east from here, before making an abrupt turn to the north,
but to the west it seems to reach a dead-end, with cold, dripping walls to every
side. In places, the brickwork seems to bulge, as if it were straining to
withstand the weight of ground and water beyond, which seem ever to be striving
to reclaim this area as their own.
name: Dead-end Tunnel

desc:
A tall shaft opens up above your head, and high on the western wall what appears
to be a small opening can be seen. Of course, it is hard to tell whether it is
indeed so small, or just very far above you. The walls are made of old bricks,
crumbling in places, and slick with a blanket of green-brown moss.
name: Bottom of a Deep Shaft

desc:
The darkness here is all-engulfing, almost tangible, and to the north and south
the progress of this narrow tunnel rapidly disappears from sight. The air is so
still and damp that it feels suffocating, but from a narrow shaft, set at the
very centre of the ceiling, a faint breath of fresh air can be felt. From
somewhere in the distance the repetitive noise of dripping water can be heard.
name: A Long, Low-Roofed Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel comes to an end here, blocked to the north by a rough brick wall. To
the south, it stretches off into the darkness, and from somewhere in the
distance the incessant plop of dripping water can be heard. Though the roof of
the passage is quite low, a narrow shaft can be seen at its centre, and from far
above comes a rumour of fresh cool air.
name: End of a Long, Low-Roofed Tunnel

desc:
A chill air pervades the chamber, and a musty odour assails your nostrils. The
room is small and rectangular, with a high ceiling and a perfectly even floor.
Three of the walls are made up of smooth blocks of stone, precisely placed, and
mortared with such care that even the seeping water has not done much damage
over the years. However, the fourth wall appears to have been constructed in
haste, with rough bricks and no mortar.
name: Dank Chamber at the Tunnel's End

desc:
This large, square chamber is rather ostentatious. The walls are draped with
heavy tapestries whose sombre hues seem to swallow light. A large midnight- blue
carpet covers much of the floor, and the centre of the room is dominated by an
imposing oak desk. Wrought iron brackets of eleborate design secure torches to
each wall, casting a soft, dim light throughout the area, and to one end of the
room, a thick velvet curtain is drawn back to reveal a massive oak bed covered
with fine linens. Half-hidden by a corner of the carpet, a fairly small square
hole has been cut into the floor against the left wall of the room, not far from
the bed.
name: Chamber of the Master Assassin

desc:
The entire area is little larger than the small arched opening which leads into
it from the chamber to the north. The roof is low, barely allowing a grown man
to stand upright, and the floorspace is dominated by a rough hole, leaving
little room to move about. From somewhere below, a slightly rank odour wafts
upwards.
name: A Dark Niche

desc:
The walls of this large chamber are made from huge blocks of stone. They have
aged well, but here and there hairline cracks can be seen, and through these
chilly water seeps slowly in, dripping to the flagstone floor, where it forms
tiny puddles. Stout oak beams support the roof of the room, but toward one end
the ceiling seems to sag, as if the weight of earth above were finally winning
its battle to bury this construction.
name: Hall of the Assassins

desc:
Though uneven in places and obviously very old, the flagstone floor of this
commodious hall has stood the test of time well. The walls are showing some
signs of their age however, and in places water seeps in slowly through hairline
cracks. The roof of the chamber is supported by stout oak beams, and only such a
hard wood could have continued to brace such a heavy burden despite the damp and
mould.
name: Hall of the Assassins

desc:
You arrive at a small landing where a couple of boats have been tethered to a
small dock. The landing is lit by a couple of torches mounted on the wall by
some metal brackets. There are quite a few footprints in the dirt on the ground
suggesting this place is used quite frequently by the criminal elements of
Tharbad.
name: A Small Landing

desc:
Tall pillars rise up out of the water. The air here is dark and gloomy with a
hint of death encompassing it like a shroud. Your lights reflect off the water
giving everything around you an evil, eerie feel. Looking at the pillars you see
that they have been carved with letters that have been worn away and you are
unable to read them.
name: The Catacombs

desc:
Tall pillars rise up out of the water. The air here is dark and gloomy with a
hint of death encompassing it like a shroud. Your lights reflect off the water
giving everything around you an evil, eerie feel. Looking at the pillars you see
that they have been carved with letters that have been worn away and you are
unable to read them.
name: The Catacombs

desc:
Tall pillars rise up out of the water. The air here is dark and gloomy with a
hint of death encompassing it like a shroud. Your lights reflect off the water
giving everything around you an evil, eerie feel. Looking at the pillars you see
that they have been carved with letters that have been worn away and you are
unable to read them.
name: The Catacombs

desc:
Tall pillars rise up out of the water. The air here is dark and gloomy with a
hint of death encompassing it like a shroud. Your lights reflect off the water
giving everything around you an evil, eerie feel. Looking at the pillars you see
that they have been carved with letters that have been worn away and you are
unable to read them.
name: The Catacombs

desc:
Tall pillars rise up out of the water. The air here is dark and gloomy with a
hint of death encompassing it like a shroud. Your lights reflect off the water
giving everything around you an evil, eerie feel. Looking at the pillars you see
that they have been carved with letters that have been worn away and you are
unable to read them.
name: The Catacombs

desc:
Tall pillars rise up out of the water. The air here is dark and gloomy with a
hint of death encompassing it like a shroud. Your lights reflect off the water
giving everything around you an evil, eerie feel. Looking at the pillars you see
that they have been carved with letters that have been worn away and you are
unable to read them.
name: The Catacombs

desc:

hint of death encompassing it like a shroud. Your lights reflect off the water
giving everything around you an evil, eerie feel. Looking at the pillars you see
that they have been carved with letters that have been worn away and you are
unable to read them.
name: The Catacombs

giving everything around you an evil, eerie feel. Looking at the pillars you see
that they have been carved with letters that have been worn away and you are
unable to read them.
name: The Catacombs

desc:

giving everything around you an evil, eerie feel. Looking at the pillars you see
that they have been carved with letters that have been worn away and you are
unable to read them.
name: The Catacombs

desc:
Tall pillars rise up out of the water. The air here is dark and gloomy with a
hint of death encompassing it like a shroud. Your lights reflect off the water
giving everything around you an evil, eerie feel. Looking at the pillars you see
that they have been carved with letters that have been worn away and you are
unable to read them.
name: The Catacombs

desc:
Tall pillars rise up out of the water. The air here is dark and gloomy with a
hint of death encompassing it like a shroud. Your lights reflect off the water
giving everything around you an evil, eerie feel. Looking at the pillars you see
that they have been carved with letters that have been worn away and you are
unable to read them.
name: The Catacombs

desc:
Tall pillars rise up out of the water. The air here is dark and gloomy with a
hint of death encompassing it like a shroud. Your lights reflect off the water
giving everything around you an evil, eerie feel. Looking at the pillars you see
that they have been carved with letters that have been worn away and you are
unable to read them.
name: The Catacombs

desc:
You arrive out of the water at quite a large landing with a couple of piers
extending out into the water. All around you there are slaves loading up small
boats with what must be contraband of some sort or other. To the east you can
see a passage lit by torches on the walls.
name: A Landing

desc:
This passage is well lit and there seems to be a lot of traffic heading up and
down the passage. Along the walls at regular intervals are torches mounted on
stout iron brackets. The passage continues east and west.
name: A Lit Passage

desc:
The passage widens a bit here and to the south you can see a large warehouse
filled with crates and boxes. Along the passage trudge slaves laden with crates
and other sundry items. There seems to be a lot of liquor and pipe-weed passing
through this area. The warehouse is to the south and the passage continues west.
name: A Lit Passage

desc:
You constantly hear the sound of grasshoppers around you. Larger plants seem to
be rare in this area. To the north you see some field with green grass, but the
land seems to become even more dry to the east.
name: Dunland Flats

desc:
The grass is not as green as maybe it should be. The landscape is beginning to
seem dry, allowing only a few plants to grow. You can see that the great Dunland
Flats stretch out in all directions around you.
name: Dunland Flats

desc:
The grass grows sparsely here. Some small bugs crawl between the blades on the
ground. Here you see the distinct tracks of many hooved animals. North and west
you see more flat land, but east you see the silhouette of a very large tree
against the horizon.
name: Dunland Flats

desc:
A gigantic tree, at least a hundred feet high is standing here. It doesn't have
any leaves, but can still give protection from the sun or the rain if you stand
on the right side of it. North and west you see flat, dry land. However, east
you see a small waterhole.
name: A Huge Tree

desc:
Some green bushes grow near this waterhole, almost hiding the remains of some
large animal. It is only the skeleton that is left. Obviously it has been lying
here for a very long time. The water in the waterhole is somewhat dark and
perfectly still. Some flies are swarming near the bushes. This waterhole must be
what's supplying the huge tree to the west with water. North and east the land
is flat and dry.
name: Waterhole

desc:
Thin grass covers the ground here along with some small plants. Once this land
probably was a fine grazing field. Small insects fly around here. There is a
hill to the north and some flat land to the east. West you see a pool of water.
name: Dunland Flats

desc:
Gradually the land becomes more and more fertile to the south. Large areas of
grassy plains and fields stretch southward far into the skyline. Here the rich
soil supports plenty of plant life and is carpeted by a layer of lush green
grass. Vegetation becomes more sparse to the north while the fertile land con-
tinues to the east.
name: A Field

desc:
The southern region of this region is fertile and lush with a thick carpet of
green grass. Numerous patches of mid-length wild grass provide ample shelter and
food for various small-sized wild animals. Rabbits especially seem to be the
predominant wild-life. Vegetation becomes sparse north of here while the fertile
land continues both east and west of here.
name: A Field

desc:
Here the Old South Road turns southward and disappears into the thick grassy
layers that stretch out to the south. It is impossible to make out the road
around the bend. Small clusters of flower and brush are scattered around the
large field. Northwards the land becomes more fertile and the flora more rich.
Eastward the road continues on its way.
name: Old South Road

desc:
A well preserved road here can be seen clearly running through the grass field.
That is probably because the road overlays the field by a few inches and no
plants have been able to take root among the closely spaced cracks. Fertile
grassy plains and fields surround the road. A barren plain lies to the east
while the road continues north and westwards.
name: Old South Road

desc:
As it steadily continues towards the lands of Gondor, the Old South Road runs in
an almost straight line. Scattered along the sides of the almost invisible road
lie the remnants of the old stone markers that indicated the road. Otherwise the
area is covered by a thick grass layer dotted with small groups of flowers.
Along the eastern side the remnants of an old stone fence can be seen while
westward lies a more fertile land. South and north of here the road continues.
name: Old South Road

desc:
The once well used road from Gondor to Fornost can barely be seen amidst the
rich flora here. More or less broken and hidden beneath the grass lie the
remnants of the old stonework that once was laid here near the intersection.
Flowers of many kinds and colours coming from different places of the area have
made their roots here. Along the the eastern side of the road what was once a
stone fence now lies in a heap of crumbled rock. The road continues to the south
while north it enters an intersection. West of here lies a flat plain.
name: Old South Road

desc:
Encouraged by the moist and fertile soil, massive looking weeds flourish and
dominate the northern part of this field. The exit to the road is almost barred
by the intertwining high growth. Southwards the weeds give over to a turf of
short sturdy wild grass. Here and there various kind of wild flowers spot the
land among small thickets of bushes. East of here is a road of some kind while
west of here the plains continue. South of here the grass seems to merge into a
flowery field.
name: Plains along the Road

desc:
The grassy field here is awash with a rainbow carpet of blooming wild flowers.
Countless species vie for attention from nectar seeking insects with bright hues
of red, white, yellow, purple and pink. East of here the faint outline of the
Old South Road can be seen while west of here lies a rather dry looking plain.
name: In a Field

desc:
Dotted across the plain are thickets of various sizes and shapes. Here the
ground is fertile and sustains a thick carpet of sturdy short wild grass.
Winding its way slowly southwards is a small rivulet. The water carries with it
an unpleasant stench. Plains stretch in all directions.
name: A Dry Plain

desc:
Here the land is dry and a bit sandy compared to the areas north of here. The
thin topsoil makes it hard for anything to grow here, but a few thorny thickets
have managed to take root. The whole region is rather flat and dreary. Far to
the northeast a structure of some sort is barely visible. The plains continue in
all directions.
name: A Dry Plain

desc:
A slight wind seems to blow perpetually around this bluff, setting loose a
little topsoil, which is readily blown away, as there is little grass here to
anchor it. The view is excellent, letting you see the endless plains to the
south and east. There is flat land in all directions, but far southwest you see
something that looks like a small pond.
name: Eroded Bluff

desc:
Dry and dusty, only allowing thin blades of grass grow in small turfs, this
steppe also shows signs of hoofed animals moving around. North and west there is
more flat landscape. East you see a bluff of some kind and there is a waterhole
to the south.
name: Dunland Flats

desc:
These plains are arid and dry. Very little grass is growing here. The top layer
of the soil is not bound to the ground by roots and lots of grass thus it's
loose. East and west you see flat land. North the land seems a bit more
vegetated, and south you see something resembling to a huge tree.
name: Dunland Flats

desc:
Small piles of dirt and soil lay here and there, violently torn up from the
ground by what seems to be some hoofed animals. The grass has been torn away,
revealing the soil upon which it used to grow. In every direction you look there
is plain, flat land.
name: Dunland Flats

desc:
Short, dry grass on the ground an occasional bush can be seen here. A small
snake hurries for cover and disappears. The vegetation seems to increase in the
north west direction and decrease to the south east.
name: Dunland Flats

desc:
The grass is low, but still quite thick. There are some small holes in the
ground which probably serve as entrance to some small animal dwelling. Where the
trail is the grass is flat against the ground and it leads north and west. East
and south you see some flat grassland.
name: Trail in the Grass

desc:
This is a field with thick, high grass which hides a small trail. The trail
makes a turn here and goes in an east-south direction. When you look around you
there is only more flat land to be seen in all directions.
name: Thick Grassy Field

desc:
Standing on the small trail, which cuts through the dense grass, you see flat
land all around you. East you see some low-grass plains and it seems as the
landscape is getting kind of arid the more south you move. You can follow the
path north or west through some more fields.
name: Trail in the Field

desc:
The ground is not very capable of providing plants and grass with nutrition and
water it seems, as it is sparsely covered. There are more plains in all
directions, but the vegetation seems just a bit more dense to the west.
name: Plains

desc:
Flat land with thin, low grass is all around you. Some insects fly by as you
turn your head around and watch the vast plains. The plains to the north seem to
have more dense grass while the ones south seems to show signs of lack of water.
West and east you also see more plains.
name: Plains

desc:
Apparently this area is favoured by bugs and insects. They seem to like crawling
on the blades of the grass and jumping to another one. The grass is short and
here and there you see a small plant. Wherever you look you see more plains, but
the southern ones seem a bit arid.
name: Plains

desc:
The grass shifts and ripples as insects jump out of your path just before you
catch a clear look at them. There is no sign of civilisation on these seemingly
endless plains, and it has perhaps been a long time since anyone chose to walk
across these lands.
name: Plains

desc:
A large wasteland marks the beginning of the flat plains that continue to the
south towards the area known as Enedhwaith. Here the plains are rather muddy due
to the murky swamp that fills the area north of the road. No vegetation
flourishes here except for a few blades of yellow sickly grass. The plains
continue to the east, west and south.
name: Plains along the Road

desc:
Thick dark mud occupies the plains here alongside the road. South of here the
plain becomes more dry and green with grass. North of here is a large swampy
area that dominates the region. Grassy rolling plains gradually flatten out to
the south. The plains continue both east and west and to the south.
name: Plains along the Road

desc:
The grass is short and well-tended. There are many different plants and flowers
growing in the flower-bed which borders the lawn. On the other side of the
flower-bed the vegetation becomes quite tangled, and going that way is
inconvenient; but there is a path which leaves the garden to the west and
plunges abruptly down a muddy bank into the dense brush.
name: A Fine Lawn

desc:
This is a small cabin which has been packed with seemingly hundreds of trophies,
bones, artefacts, "treasures", pieces of driftwood, skulls, polished stones, and
pieces of nautical equipment of known and unknown functions. Most of these
things seem water-worn, chipped, cracked, or rusted, and none of it has any
apparent value; but it has all been carefully sorted, cleaned, and securely
mounted on every available inch of wall space, with the overflow nailed to the
rafters overhead. The owner of this cabin has apparently hauled this entire
trove out of the river, piece by piece, through years of proud, if pointless,
industry.
name: Small Cottage

desc:
The vegetation here is mostly reeds and grass, with some clumps of bushes near
muddy sloughs. North and west of here it grows even dense, suggesting a river or
lake lies in that direction. A path rises up a steep bank to the east, coming
from there. Looking south you see more of this bushy, wet land.
name: Brushy Grassland

desc:
Stretching out wide in all directions are the deserted fields and plains of the
once so great Eregion. But now there is little left that beholds the life and
culture that once was. The fields continue to the east while to the west the Old
South Road can barely be seen. North of here is a more fertile land.
name: Waste Plains

desc:
The thick green carpet of grass here is evidence of the rich fertile soil of
this region. Scattered around are flowers, bushes and even some trees. North and
east of here the fertile grass covered fields continue while the vegetation
thins out into a barren plain to the south. To the west remains of the Old South
Road are barely visible. Remnants of the once surrounding stonefence lie to the
south and west.
name: A Cultivated Area

desc:
Here among the grass and flowers grow small patches of tall wild wheat. Maybe
the only testimony to the long gone inhabitants who used to plant wheat fields
in this area. An old ruin lies to the north while the fields continue to the
south and east. The remains of the Old South Road can be reached to the west.
name: A Cultivated Area

desc:
This is the remnant of a once cultivated field. Where once various crops used to
grow, many wild flowers and weeds have sprung up. The field carries on to the
south. The field slowly turns into a grassy plain as it starts to creep on
towards the east. To the north an old elven road, leading to Ost-in-Edhil, can
be spotted.
name: An Old Field

desc:
A thick, green layer of grass stretches out in all directions before you. West
and northward the grassy field merges into fertile areas filled with various
flowers and wild weeds. To the south and east the grassy field begins to change
into rolling plains.
name: A Field

desc:
This is the beginning of the great plains that start to stretch themselves out
southwards towards Dunfearan and Enedhwaith. Grass covered hills and thickets
can be spotted decorating the rolling landscape. The plains continue onwards
towards both the east and west, while to the north fertile fields can be seen.
Off to the east is a tiny hill.
name: Plains

desc:
Here someone has dug out a rather large room. The roof and walls are supported
by stone pillars which seem to have withstood the passage of time remarkably
well. Thick layers of earth and dust cover the floor, evidence of the long
vacancy of this room. Scattered on the floor are the remnants of what were once
shining armours and well forged swords. Westwards lies a dark corridor.
name: Under the Altar

desc:
It is dark here inside the old ancient corridor. Supporting the roof here are
some large marble pillars which seem remarkably well-kept. All the pillars have
been decorated by old elven runes and strange carvings. The air is rather heavy
and almost tinged with the presence of the old noldorian power. East of here
lies a rather large room dug out of the earth while west of here the corridor
continues.
name: In the Corridor

desc:
Here the corridor makes a rather sharp bend from the east to the north. The
supporting pillars lining the corridor shows signs of decaying but the runes and
carvings are still visible. The black and moist earthen floor seems to consume
the light. East and north of here the corridor continues.
name: In the Corridor

desc:
Here the corridor has come to an end. You are now standing in a small square
room, the walls and floor covered with damp. The damp has to come from the
swamps that lie many feet above you. Examining the room you notice it has one
marble pillar in each corner and at the western wall you can see something that
resembles a fireplace.
name: End of the Corridor

desc:
Neatly carved into the wall is a small and narrow niche. Just like the other
rooms, here the floor is also covered with thick layers of dirt and dust. The
only thing of interest here is a large dust-covered chest. The chest is made out
of richly decorated iron. Much of the decor is intricately carved elven runes
known as Cirth.
name: A Small Niche

desc:
The lake is filled with dark, deep and ice cold water here. It is extremely dark
here, and the distance between the water and the roof is not much at all. The
distance is just enough for a person to catch his breath without knocking his
head against the roof. Water drips from the roof into the lake, and the walls
are damp and slimy.
name: Underground Lake

desc:
The lake bends north and west from here. To the north the lake becomes shallow
and seems to end up in a tunnel leading north. Eastwards the lake becomes deeper
and the distance between the roof and the water is very short. Water drips from
the roof, and the walls are damp and slimy. A cold draft blows here, which makes
the water ice cold.
name: Bend in the Lake

desc:
On this small plateau, a few feet above the water, the ground is cold but dry.
The water below seems dark and cold, but rather shallow as it continues
southwards from here. Damp, cold walls and a roof made of stone fill this place
with darkness. There are some cracks in the walls, but still the smoothness of
them indicate that it was people who made this place rather than wild animals or
the passage of water. The tunnel continues northwards into the darkness.
name: Underground Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel comes to an end here. This place seems to be rather dry, and some
dirt can be seen on the ground. It's very dark here and the distance between the
roof and the floor is not even high enough for a normal human to stand up
properly. The tunnel slopes downwards to the south, and the sound of water can
be heard in the distance.
name: End of the Tunnel

desc:
The walls are dry here as well as the floor. A damp and rotting smell fills the
air and a few drops of water are dripping from the roof. Some marks and cracks
can be seen on the southern and eastern walls. On the eastern wall there is a
large slit that a person, with some effort, could possibly squeeze out through.
name: Crypt

desc:
This room appears to be abandoned. All the furniture is made of rotting wood,
and a damp fleshy smell fills the air. The dark tomb, with its floor and walls
covered in dirt looks like it was abandoned years ago. The smooth walls and the
furniture indicates that this place was made by cunning people. On the northern
wall there is a door made of stone, covered with spiderwebs.
name: The Tomb

desc:
The thick, soft grass which grows here makes travel very difficult. In many
places the grass has grown to several feet in height. An occasional pine tree
can be seen sprouting from the ground. The plains continue in every direction.
name: Plains

desc:
The outlines of a faint path are marked with small weathered stones. The path is
easy to follow despite the weeds and small bushes covering it as the trail
travels in a fairly straight line towards the north and south. Large plains span
out from the trail to the east and west.
name: A Faint Path

desc:
Small weathered stones mark the faint outlines of a path. Small bushes and weeds
grow throughout the area, making the path of the trail difficult to follow. The
trail continues to both the north and south. Barren plains span outwards towards
both the east and west.
name: A Faint Path

desc:
Thick underbrush has been planted on both sides of the path, making the trail
easily visible. The trail begins to decay some as it heads towards the north as
the underbrush recedes. A small hill rises above the plains to the west. The
trail continues onwards to the east in the direction of some ruined buildings.
name: Bend in the Faint Path

desc:
This old shelter sure has seen better times. The walls have fallen in and all
the woodwork has rotted away. Only the northern wall seems to still be going
strong. Here someone has cleared away the rubble, and the remains of a campfire
can be seen. Left in the corner is some dry wood covered with a brown looking
fur. From here you can go west. Obviously hidden with great haste, you can also
see an old chest among the stone rubble.
name: A Downcast Shelter

desc:
The plains rise towards a tiny hill, covered in lush, green grass. Clusters of
flowers and brushwood dot the landscape. A ring of small stones once used for a
fire pit can be spied to the south. The plains continue onwards towards the
north and west. The outline of a faint path is to the east.
name: A Tiny Hill

desc:
Many grasses, weeds, and flowers grow in the fertile earth. A few young pine
trees and an occasional bush also dot the landscape. The land begins to slope
downward towards a ruined field to the north. Water marks in the ground seem to
indicate that excess water from these fields have drained to the ruined field
below. The land continues to rise gradually towards a tiny hill to the south.
The grassy fields stretch out to the west. The faint outline of an old path lies
to the east.
name: Plains

desc:
The fields in this area are submerged under a thin layer of water, making it
very difficult for most types of vegetation to grow. Only a few brown weeds and
gray reeds can be seen above the water line. The lands to the west and south
begin to slope upwards towards drier ground. A seldom used path can be spied to
the east, leading away from the fields. An old road passes by to the north,
muddy from excess water from the fields draining on to it.
name: A Flooded Field

desc:
The green ocean of grass that engulfs the area is only broken up by occasional
dense thickets of thornbushes and several pine trees. The plains continue on in
all directions except for to the west, where the faint outline of an old path
can be spied.
name: Plains

desc:
Less extravagant than the larger chamber adjoining it, this room seems to be of
a strictly utilitarian design. The floorboards are bare of carpeting, and the
torches mounted upon each wall are held in the simplest of iron sconces.
Nevertheless it is apparent that this room sees frequent use; scuffed foot-
prints disturb the thin later of sawdust covering the ground, and the air hangs
heavy with the odour of sweat.
name: In a Cramped Antechamber

desc:
This small room is dominated by an old spiral staircase, circling upwards. The
staircase appears old, covered with rust and webs. Though it might support the
weight of one person, several people climbing the stairs at once might find the
structure breaking under their combined weight. Apart from the staircase, the
room appears to be featureless and reveals little of interest.
name: Bottom of a Secret Stair

desc:
A spiral staircase twists its way down into the utter darkness below. Though
rusted and apparently not often used, the stairs seem to be stable enough to
support the weight of a single person. However, should a whole group of
explorers expose it to their combined weight, they might very well find
themselves plunging down to be crushed far below.
name: Top of a Secret Staircase

desc:
The corridor stretches, straight as an arrow, from north to south, its floor
perfectly level, and its walls parallel. Empty brackets hang in rows to either
side, but further along the passageway a faint, flickering torchlight can be
seen. The ceiling is beaded with a few drops of water, especially on the east
side of the hallway, but otherwise the tunnel seems sound, and heavy wooden
doors, to the right and left, rumour the presence of chambers beyond.
name: In a Long, Dark Corridor

desc:
The walls of this large, echoing cellar are cracked, damp and moldy in some
places, and the once hard-packed earth floor is now a muddy morass. The stagnant
air is thick with the stench of rotting organic matter and heavy with moisture.
It would seem that the river and the creeping marshes are intent upon
undermining the foundations of the small building above.
name: Muddy Cellar

desc:
The interior of this building is a single rectangular room of uninspiring design
and proportions. Four narrow, slitted openings can be seen: two to each side of
the heavy oak door in the western wall. A model of the most spartan
functionality with plain stone walls and a well-worn stone floor, this little
building was obviously constructed to serve as little more than a sentry box to
look over the approaches to the gates of Tharbad.
name: Inside a Small Stone Building

desc:
This large, low-roofed chamber clearly serves as both bedroom and living
quarters for a number of people. Narrow bunks of spartan design line two walls,
and a long table runs between them, with sturdy wooden benches to either side. A
number of simple cabinets stand against the far wall, and the room is
illuminated by the light of some foul-smelling tapers.
name: Austere Lodgings

desc:
The corridor comes to an end in front of an imposing double door of burnished
black metal. The walls, floor and ceiling here are made of huge slabs of smooth
grey stone which sparkle with tiny flecks of mica; high on each wall, wrought
iron brackets hold large torches which cast an eerie, dancing light into the
darkness of the corridor beyond.
name: Before the Double Doors

desc:
Beyond the narrow and well-hidden doorway lies a small room, whose walls,
covered with mortar, show here and there discoloured patches of mold. In the
middle of the room stands an imposing marble table, supporting what seems to be
an alchemist's set of tools: heavy stone mortars, glass vials, beakers, all more
or less dirty with the remains of a former concoction. Several small jars, none
of which are labelled, can be seen on a long shelf fixed to the southern wall. A
large tome, seemingly well-used, is fixed to the table with a heavy iron chain.
name: Small Laboratory

desc:
An iron staircase spirals away, above and below you. The wooden handrail is
completely rotten in places, and the superstructure and treads are covered with
rust and cobwebs. Though the stair might support the weight of one quite safely,
it looks as if it might perhaps give way under a greater load.
name: On an Unstable Stair

desc:
This small round chamber is featureless save for two arched openings in the
perfectly curved wall. One of these appears to lead to another room, but the
other, tiny, and partly blocked with rough planks of wood, seems to let onto
little more than a cramped alcove. The flat ceiling of the chamber is about
twenty-five feet above the flagstone floor, and as the walls are perfectly
smooth there is no way to reach the square hole, which can be seen directly
above the larger archway.
name: A Small Empty Chamber

desc:
This vast room is cold, damp and musty. Shelves line the walls to either side of
you, but the wall opposite the door is so damp that nothing appears to be stored
there any longer. A few meat hooks hang from the ceiling not far from the door,
but it is clear this chamber has to serve many purposes other than food storage,
for an odd collection of objects is scattered on the floor and shelves.
name: A Large Echoing Storeroom

desc:
This is less of a room and more of a niche. It is no more than about six feet
across in either direction, and the curved ceiling sweeps down toward the floor
on all sides, meaning that only at the very centre of the room can anyone of
average human stature stand upright. The floor is made from robust wooden
boards, with a square trapdoor set just to one side of the centre of the
chamber.
name: A Cramped Nook

desc:
A small cabin has been built inside a massive, hollowed out trunk. The walls are
of living wood, and have been smoothened from the inside. Small carvings can be
seen on the walls and a few pieces of furniture are placed in the centre of the
room. The floor has been constructed out of sturdy planks. There is a trapdoor
with a keyhole on the floor, and a round hole on the southern wall allowing a
way out. There is no ceiling; where the hollow space ends above, the trunk
continues.
name: Hideout inside a Tree

desc:
High in the branches of a massive tree, thick gnarled boughs form a small
platform. The numerous branches are devoid of leaves, allowing the wind to pick
up and whip around at this height. To the north there is a peculiar-looking hole
in the side of the tree trunk. Below lies a small garden with plentiful small
flowers and vegetables.
name: On a Branch

desc:
Lush grass, flowers and other small plants cover this garden sheltered inside
the shrubbery. A giant tree grows in the middle of the opening, and its thick
and rough branches form natural ladders which can be used to climb out. All
other exits through the intact wall of vegetation look impassable.
name: Overgrown Garden

desc:
The water is still.. The current in the river doesn't seem to affect it down
here at this side, making the soil washed here by the river form a soft,
swamp-like bottom. The dirt that is floating around here keeps the light from
the surface out so it looks dead and calm down here.
name: The River Bottom

desc:
The flow of the river is slow here, causing dirt and debris to sink to the
bottom. A vague structure can be outlined in the mud, but whether it is manmade
or just a heap of debris is hard to tell.
name: The River Bottom

desc:
Smooth, hard mud covers the bottom here. Very little vegetation has managed to
get a foothold in the swift current. The featurelessness of the bottom leaves
little to hold on to, as you drift westward.
name: The River Bottom

desc:
Bright sand covers most of the bottom around here. The water flows slowly and is
relatively clear. The south bank is entangled by roots, and the river gets more
shallow.
name: The River Bottom

desc:
The current is swift here, pulling you westward. The bottom consists mainly of
mixed hard mud, sand, and rounded pebbles. Little catches your eye, as the river
carries any debris past this place.
name: The River Bottom

desc:
The river bottom is of smooth, hard mud here. As the current is swift it has
scoured it clean of any vegetation. A few bits of rusty iron have sunk here, but
you cannot tell what they are.
name: The River Bottom

desc:
You are inside an old ship wreck, preserved by the peat bog surrounding it. Most
parts of it have, during the many years it must have been here, collapsed or got
overgrown by different underwater plants. To the south, however, seems to be a
sturdy section, made of reinforced oak. There's a door leading south there, that
is also made of reinforced oak.
name: In the Wreck

desc:
This must have been the captain's room, judging by the once luxurious furniture
in here. Not much has survived staying in the water for a such long time as it
must have been. You see remainders of a table and what must have been a bed. In
a corner lies a pile of bones from a human.
name: The Captain's Cabin

desc:
As you enter the hold of this ship, you are almost blinded by the filthy, dark,
and still water. All wood and timber has rotted, as have the barrels that once
might have held food, water, or sail-cloth. A greenish, sediment- covered
obstruction traverses the hull from south to north.
name: In the Hold of an Ancient Wreck

desc:
This is a cramped and formless crevice or vacuity between two rotting hulls,
wrecked long ago and buried by debris. The water in this space is so filthy with
rotten wood and slime that it is like swimming in thin mud. Little shapes dart
about... they might be fish, or they might not.
name: Between Two Hulls

desc:
This is a water-filled vertical shaft whose earthen walls seem about ready to
fall in at any moment. The water is muddy and foul-tasting; and it is about the
darkest place you have ever seen.
name: A Flooded Shaft

desc:
This is a stifling, damp underground shaft, about three feet across. Its walls
are made of crumbling earth; above, it is capped with a stony substance. Worms
and little bugs crawl out of the earth. Below is a surface of black, filthy
water.
name: A Narrow Shaft

desc:
This almost shapeless vacuity, bounded by rotten wood, mud, peat, and roots, is
barely recognisable as the lower deck and hold of a small cargo vessel. The
blackened planks crumble softly at your touch; the crumbs of wood drift slowly
in the black and stagnant water. Shapeless debris is everywhere. Dim shapes seem
to slither at the limits of your vision.
name: A Sunken, Buried Wreck
